findings.title: Canonical permission example over-grants before least-authority reduction
findings.evidence: SKILL.md presents a canonical starter permission object with write, edit, bash, memory, list, and external_directory all set to allow, while the same contract later says permissions must be explicit least-authority choices and the permission reference says external_directory is high-risk and should be denied unless clearly needed.
findings.recommendation: Make the SKILL.md starter block explicitly illustrative, or change high-risk/default-unneeded permissions to deny/ask and instruct authors to elevate only with role-specific proof.
findings.resolution: Applied: canonical starter block external_directory changed allow->deny at SKILL.md, aligning it with the block's own deny of the other high-risk tools (webfetch, chrome_devtools, patch) and permission_design.md high-risk-deny rule. Authors elevate with role-specific justification per frontmatter rule 5.
findings.scopeProof: Reviewed SKILL.md permission block and permission_design.md permission rules; no other active P0/P1 issue was supported by validation or link checks.
